mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates strong labor demand and consumer income growth, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This signal is always active as a leading indicator of economic expansion.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return conditional on a positive PAYEMS change is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the 12-month change in CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, it signals persistent inflation that prompts the Fed to tighten monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In occasions when the trigger is active, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 55% of the time, contradicting the negative prediction.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of low unemployment (UNRATE below 4%) and rising Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS increasing) defines a late-cycle macro environment where tightening monetary policy meets a tight labor market, historically leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (UNRATE * FEDFUNDS) in a multivariate regression on 20-day forward returns fails to achieve statistical significance (p<0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During the early-cycle recovery phase, a positive surprise in quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) reinforces the expansion narrative and boosts equity sentiment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This effect reverses in late-cycle periods.
failure_condition: In identified early-cycle periods (e.g., NBER dated troughs plus 12 months), the Rank IC of GDPC1 growth on 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 or the direction is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive monthly change in industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity and economic momentum, which is immediately reflected in rising equity prices over the following week (5 trading days). This is a high-frequency leading indicator.
failure_condition: The mean 5-day forward return following a positive INDPRO change is not greater than the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.05) or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1.5 million units (annualized), it reflects strong residential construction activity and consumer confidence, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as the market anticipates a thriving housing sector.
failure_condition: In periods when HOUST > 1500, the 5-day forward return is negative more often than positive (hit rate < 50%) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising ratio of retail sales (RSAFS) to industrial production (INDPRO) indicates that consumer demand is outpacing supply, creating inflationary bottlenecks that erode corporate margins and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction between these two series amplifies the signal.
failure_condition: The predictive power of the RSAFS/INDPRO ratio fails in cross-validation: the Spearman correlation with 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p>0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In the regime where the Fed is exiting the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS starting to rise from near zero), the initial tightening steps cause a repricing of risk premiums and a negative equity market reaction as investors adjust to the removal of accommodation. This effect is specific to the zero-lower-bound exit regime.
failure_condition: In episodes where FEDFUNDS rises from below 0.25% to above 0.25% (exit condition), the 60-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, contradicting the negative prediction.
